title: DIGITAL TWIN VALIDATION FOR SHOULDER JOINT BIOMECHANICAL SIMULATION
authors: Fernández-Álvarez J.J.; Roces-García J.; Lamela-Rey M.J.; Pena-Vázquez J.
abstract: Glenohumeral joint is the main responsible of the shoulder’s movement. In this moment, Shoulder arthroplasty is increasing rapidly [1] to treat both arthritis and bone fractures. However, there are limited studies about their biomechanical behaviour. The number of studies reduces even more if reverse shoulder prosthesis, a type of prosthesis in which the component that recreates the humeral head is placed in the glenosphere, are considered. After the placement of these prosthesis to solve a fracture, usually in four fragments, some cases of reduced bone density (bone remodelling) have been noted, causing long term problems due to the loss of muscular insertions. In this context, this document presents a methodology to generate digital twins of the shoulder joint from clinic images [2], prepared to perform finite element (FE) simulations on which the prosthesis can be included and perform virtual surgeries. The methodology starts with clinical images (Argus CT, Sedecal Molecular Imaging SLU®) of the shoulder joint, which enable surface detailed models with the program 3D SLICER. These models are imported into the program SPACECLAIM developed by ANSYS® for their debugging, their conversion into solid models and their combination with the prosthesis. After that procedure, the meshing of these models is generated. In order to assign the bone’s mechanical properties, a program named BONEMAT is used. In this program, the meshes of the model and the images are imported and the program assigns to every node of the mesh the mechanical properties of the Houndsfield units of the images. The mechanical properties of the prosthesis’ mesh are defined by their manufactured material. The last step in this methodology consists in reintroducing the meshes into ANSYS [3], applying the boundary conditions and performing the simulation. This FE simulations have been validated with a lab test performed on pork bones with a reverse shoulder prosthesis (INTEGRA Titan Reverse Shoulder Prosthesis), on which a load test has been performed, using a triaxial testing machine Walter & Bai. To do so, the bone have been tomographied and applying the methodology previously described, the displacement was calculated under the conditions of the lab test (figure 1). (Figure Presented) The data was acquired from the control system of the testing machine (force and head displacement) which were compared with the simulated tests. The analysis shows a difference between the real and simulated tests lower than 15%. The main conclusion obtained from this work is that performance simulations of the shoulder joint are possible. To allow this procedure, a partnership between medical personnel and engineers is critical. The results of these simulations can help the medical team with their predictions of the behaviour of the joint, possible problems with internal stresses, bone remodelling and the degree of integration of the prosthesis in the patient. © 2022 Publicaciones Dyna Sl. All rights reserved.

title: Enhancing structural damage evaluation of PC girder bridges through digital twinning Bayesian model updating
authors: Chen D.; Kim C.-W.; Yoshida E.; Sim C.
abstract: Develops a physics-based digital-twin framework for PC girder bridges using Bayesian model selection and updating, a high-fidelity 3D finite-element model, and tendon-damage simulation. The updated model is validated against destructive experiments using modal frequencies, crack patterns, and load-deflection behavior.
selection_note: Added from the 39+2 transferable-method table as a non-production digital-twin validation example with measurement/experiment comparison and calibration/update.

title: Reusing Model Validation Methods for the Continuous Validation of Digital Twins of Cyber-Physical Systems
abstract: Proposes reuse of model-validation metrics for continuous validation of simulation-based digital twins, detecting anomalies in twinned cyber-physical systems and correcting the digital twin through parameter estimation. Demonstrated on an academic but industrially relevant gantry-crane case study.
selection_note: Added from the 39+2 transferable-method table as a continuous-validation example for cyber-physical digital twins.

title: Real-time Validation of Digital Models for Manufacturing Systems: a Novel Signal-processing-based Approach
authors: Lugaresi G.; Aglio G.; Folgheraiter F.; Matta A.
source_title: 2019 IEEE 15th International Conference on Automation Science and Engineering (CASE)
abstract: Develops a real-time validation procedure for manufacturing digital models based on signal-processing theory and quasi trace-driven simulation, allowing validity checks in short-term manufacturing decision horizons without large historical datasets.
selection_note: Added from the 39+2 transferable-method table as a production-system real-time validation example with online/continuous and time-series/signal comparison logic.

title: Online geometry assurance in individualized production by feedback control and model calibration of digital twins
authors: Sjöberg A.; Önnheim M.; Frost O.; Cronrath C.; Gustavsson E.; Lennartson B.; Jirstrand M.
abstract: Presents online calibration of a digital twin for sheet-metal assembly using feedback from simulated assembly quality compared with digital-twin prediction, combined with Kalman filtering and one-step look-ahead optimization for individualized control.
selection_note: Added from the 39+2 transferable-method table as a production/resource-level geometry-assurance validation and calibration example.

title: A modeling and adaptive evolution method for simulation parameters of digital twin shop floor
authors: Zhang L.; Guo Y.; Wang S.; Zheng G.; Qian W.; Huang S.; Fang W.
abstract: Integrates deep learning with discrete-event simulation for digital twin shop-floor parameter evolution. A sequential regression variational autoencoder models time-varying simulation parameters, and an online instructor updates the model from online data to maintain simulation accuracy.
selection_note: Added as the requested Zhang et al. 2026 exception from the 39+2 transferable-method table; production-system shop-floor adaptive parameter evolution.

title: Digital twin - based model updating method for mechanical behaviors analysis of cable truss structure
authors: Shi G.; Liu Z.; Lu D.; Zhang Q.; Wang Z.; Zhao Y.
abstract: High-fidelity finite-element digital twin of a cable-truss structure with parameter-correction model updating for mechanical-behavior analysis. Experimental validation compares updated-model responses with a cable-truss test structure.
selection_note: Added from the 39+2 transferable-method table as a non-production structural DT validation example with FE model updating and measurement/calibration evidence.

title: How to Use and Derive Stylized Facts for Validating Simulation Models
source_title: Computer Simulation Validation: Fundamental Concepts, Methodological Frameworks, and Philosophical Perspectives
abstract: Discusses stylized facts as a basis for validating simulation models, including output, input, and theory validation, and proposes more systematic derivation of stylized facts for simulation-model validation.
selection_note: Added as the generic simulation-model validation reference restored from Table 2 commit 6f8a48d1ddaee735e8c0f3476fe9c0c28d39b708; used as a transferable methodological example for simulation/baseline validation.
